description_long: This object is a glass jar terrarium with a rounded body and a wide neck, sealed with a flat cork lid. Inside, a lush mound of soil supports a miniature landscape featuring several small green plants, moss, and a few visible stones. The soil is layered, with distinct strata of dark earth, lighter substrate, and a base of fine gravel or sand, all visible through the transparent glass. The plants appear healthy and vibrant, with a mix of broad and fine leaves, creating a naturalistic, self-contained ecosystem. The cork lid fits snugly, helping to maintain humidity and protect the contents. The overall appearance is clean, decorative, and suitable for display in modern urban environments.
description: A glass jar terrarium with a cork lid, containing layered soil, stones, and several small green plants. The transparent jar showcases a miniature, self-contained ecosystem.
description_view_0: Front view shows the cork lid, glass jar, and a clear view of the layered soil and central mound with plants and stones.
description_view_1: This view highlights the tallest plant and the arrangement of stones and moss, with the foliage more prominent and centered.
description_view_2: Side view reveals the profile of the mound, with leafy plants extending to the right and moss visible on the left.
description_view_3: This angle shows more of the moss and the rear foliage, with the layered soil and rocks still visible through the glass.
attribute_plant_species: mixed moss and small foliage plants (e.g., Fittonia, fern, or similar terrarium species)
